title: Delivery Tags, Acknowledgements, and Redelivery · Independent Go Trial
summary: An independent Go integration team receives different topology, traffic, schema, tenant, dependency, and shutdown constraints and transfers the module into a new a same-channel terminal delivery decision record. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a same-channel terminal delivery decision record with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose manual or automatic acknowledgement from loss tolerance, consumer memory, work duration, and effect ownership; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Acknowledge, nack, or reject a delivery exactly once on its owning channel and prevent stale or cross-channel tag use; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Transfer ownership only after the intended durable effect, transactional inbox record, or safely repeatable handoff succeeds; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Manual Auto Ack Tradeoff
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go messaging team handles case 5281 during bounded worker pool during a dependency slowdown. Build deterministic Go evidence to enforce same-generation exactly-one terminal delivery decision after durable effect; keep message identity out of metric labels while preserving causal traces; then introduce a keyed sequence gap during single-active failover. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Choose manual or automatic acknowledgement from loss tolerance, consumer memory, work duration, and effect ownership; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

What must be established before implementation?

For RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Manual Auto Ack Tradeoff, keep Manual Auto Ack Tradeoff connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

hints: For RabbitMQ and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Ack Race Single Owner, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Ack Race Single Owner precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RabbitMQ and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Ack Race Single Owner, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A lab Go messaging team handles case 4999 during stream-versus-queue architecture board. Build deterministic Go evidence to enforce same-generation exactly-one terminal delivery decision after durable effect; bound payload, queue, confirm-ledger, and handler bytes; then remove the parking binding and expose the remediation gap. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use one terminal delivery decision owner to prevent timeout, abort, handler, and shutdown paths from double acknowledging or conflicting; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

For RabbitMQ and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Ack Race Single Owner, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Calling ack twice is idempotent and safely ignored by RabbitMQ. In Go, delivery tags are globally reusable after reconnect.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Ack Race Single Owner
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go messaging team handles case 3380 during Go publisher supervisor losing one state notification. Build deterministic Go evidence to enforce same-generation exactly-one terminal delivery decision after durable effect; reject stale connection and channel generations; then lose a quorum member and recompute majority availability. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use one terminal delivery decision owner to prevent timeout, abort, handler, and shutdown paths from double acknowledging or conflicting; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

One precondition fails while output still looks plausible. What is the correct response?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Ack Race Single Owner.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use one terminal delivery decision owner to prevent timeout, abort, handler, and shutdown paths from double acknowledging or conflicting; in go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence. For RabbitMQ and Go Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Ack Race Single Owner, keep Ack Race Single Owner connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

content.title: Fault-isolation decision
content.text: Select evidence that identifies a cause, not a patch that only hides the Ack Race Single Owner symptom.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: RabbitMQ Consumer Acknowledgements and Publisher Confirms — Controls delivery tags, ack modes, requeue, prefetch, redelivery, confirm timing, multi-ack, data safety, and common client errors.

stimulus.title: Ack Race Single Owner failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Calling ack twice is idempotent and safely ignored by RabbitMQ. In Go, delivery tags are globally reusable after reconnect.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use one terminal delivery decision owner to prevent timeout, abort, handler, and shutdown paths from double acknowledging or conflicting; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Ack Race Single Owner condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.
For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient Ack Race Single Owner happy path, omit ownership, error, changed input, goroutine stop, and full-toolchain checks, then accept plausible output.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Defend the transferred model · Lab Ack Race Single Owner
steps.instruction:
Write the lab evidence note for Ack Race Single Owner: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A lab Go messaging team handles case 1761 during bounded worker pool during a dependency slowdown. Build deterministic Go evidence to enforce same-generation exactly-one terminal delivery decision after durable effect; separate deterministic browser proof from race-enabled and live-broker evidence; then close the owning channel before one delivery decision. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use one terminal delivery decision owner to prevent timeout, abort, handler, and shutdown paths from double acknowledging or conflicting; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Defend the transferred model · Lab Ack Race Single Owner.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For RabbitMQ and Go Defend the transferred model · Lab Ack Race Single Owner, keep Ack Race Single Owner connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use one terminal delivery decision owner to prevent timeout, abort, handler, and shutdown paths from double acknowledging or conflicting; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
